pi_items.source: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers in chinook.db returns 59
Task directory structure and setup_task.sh confirm path creation logic is correct. Path follows expected DBeaver export directory convention.
PRAGMA table_info(customers) confirms all 4 columns exist and are NOT NULL
PRAGMA table_info(customers) ordered by column ID exactly matches metadata list
export_result.sh line 48-49 enforces exact path matching only. setup_task.sh creates exports directory at exact expected path. verifier.py lines 80-109 check EXACT path requirement.
export_result.sh lines 96-123 validates actual customer records from database (8 known customers hardcoded). Verifier.py requires minimum 5 customers matched to prevent keyword stuffing.
Web search confirms GitHub repo; chinook database documentation at https://www.sqlitetutorial.net/sqlite-sample-database/ describes it as sample database with real iTunes data for music catalog

pi_items.verified_value: Sample customers verified: Customer 1 (Luís Gonçalves, luisg@embraer.com.br), Customer 2 (Leonie Köhler, leonekohler@surfeu.de), Customer 3 (François Tremblay, ftremblay@gmail.com), Customer 59 (Puja Srivastava, puja_srivastava@yahoo.in) all match database
pi_items.source: Direct SQLite query: SELECT CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email FROM customers WHERE CustomerId IN (1,2,3,59)

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database contains exactly 59 customers with the 13 specified columns (CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Company, Address, City, State, Country, PostalCode, Phone, Fax, Email, SupportRepId) in the exact order claimed. All 4 required columns (CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email) are present and non-nullable. The database is a publicly available sample dataset from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database based on real iTunes library data. The task enforces strict path matching (/home/ga/Documents/exports/customers_export.csv) and strict content validation by checking for actual customer records from the database. Sample customer records (IDs 1, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30, 50, 59) were verified against the actual database with matching names and email addresses.
